intro_script: Welcome back to the Daily Botcast for June 27, 2026! We're covering twelve essential updates. Today we dive into LLMs improving robot understanding, a major funding boost using game data, and new breakthroughs in robotic dexterity. Let’s go!
outro_script: And that’s your robotics rundown for June 27th! Thanks for letting us decode the circuits with you today. Until next time, keep those smart instructions coming!

items.title: LLMs help robots understand vague instructions and focus on key details
items.summary: MIT developed a novel system utilizing two language models for improving robotic task execution. The first language model's function is to interpret vague human instructions into clearer commands. Subsequently, the second model helps robots filter out extraneous or irrelevant details from those complex instructions. This dual-model approach enhances robot understanding, allowing them to focus precisely on key actions required by the user. The technology aims to make robots more capable assistants in domestic and industrial settings.
items.audio_script: From MIT News - Robotics, "LLMs help robots understand vague instructions and focus on key details." The report details a novel system designed to improve how robots execute tasks based on natural human language input. Researchers developed this dual-language model approach which first interprets ambiguous spoken commands into structured, clearer directives. Following this interpretation, a second specialized language model filters the resulting complex instructions, systematically removing extraneous or irrelevant procedural information. This methodology allows the robotic system to concentrate solely on the critical actions specified by the user. The technology indicates a potential advancement for integrating robots as more capable assistants within both domestic and industrial operational environments going forward.

items.title: General Intuition raises $320M to use video game data to train robots
items.summary: General Intuition successfully raised \$320 million in funding. This capital will be specifically utilized to advance robotic training methods. Their unique approach involves leveraging extensive video game clips for AI development. These clips contain embedded action labels, which greatly accelerates the learning process for robots. This method promises a faster and more efficient way to train sophisticated autonomous systems.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "General Intuition raises $320M to use video game data to train robots." The report details a significant funding round securing capital intended for advanced robotic training methodologies. General Intuition utilized its new investment specifically to refine how artificial intelligence learns motor skills. The core of the company's approach involves incorporating vast datasets drawn from video games, which contain detailed embedded action labels that dramatically expedite the learning curve for autonomous systems compared to traditional methods. This funding marks a notable industry step toward more rapid and scalable development of complex robotic capabilities.

items.title: ABB Robotics and Psyonic use human-generated data to advance robotic dexterity
items.summary: ABB Robotics and California bionics company Psyonic are collaborating to enhance robotic gripping and dexterity. Their new approach leverages real-world manipulation data collected from human prosthetic usage. This integration aims to overcome the significant industrial challenge of limited robotic handling capability. The partnership combines Psyonic's Ability Hand with ABB's GoFa robot arm. Improving these capabilities is expected to reduce engineering time by as much as 30%.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "ABB Robotics and Psyonic use human-generated data to advance robotic dexterity." This report details a new collaboration aimed at significantly improving the gripping capability of industrial robots through the integration of human usage data. The partnership combines ABB's GoFa robot arm with Psyonic’s Ability Hand technology. This methodology leverages real-world manipulation data derived specifically from the use of human prosthetics to enhance robotic dexterity, addressing longstanding limitations in automated handling. Industry experts anticipate that incorporating this form of bio-inspired data processing will lead to a reduction in overall engineering time for similar automation projects by up to thirty percent. The development marks an advancement toward more nuanced and adaptable robotic manipulation systems across various industrial applications.

items.title: Kawasaki Robotics showcases 8-axis Physical AI robot and intelligent automation technologies at Automate 2026
items.summary: Kawasaki Robotics displayed advanced automation technologies at Automate 2026 in Chicago. The showcase highlighted how robotics, AI, machine learning, and vision systems are transforming industry. A major feature was the unveiling of the new "RL030N" robot platform. This platform boasts 8 Degrees of Freedom (DoF) and is specifically designed for Physical AI applications. The company also showcased its patented Pulseboard technology among its offerings.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Kawasaki Robotics showcases 8-axis Physical AI robot and intelligent automation technologies at Automate 2026." The report details the company's presentation of advanced robotic solutions integrating artificial intelligence into industrial processes. At the event in Chicago, Kawasaki demonstrated how a confluence of robotics, machine learning algorithms, and vision systems is fundamentally changing manufacturing capabilities. A key element of the display was the introduction of the RL030N platform, an 8-Degrees of Freedom system engineered for sophisticated Physical AI use cases. Furthermore, patented Pulseboard technology was showcased as part of the comprehensive technological offering. These developments signal a clear industry trend toward highly adaptable and physically intelligent automated systems entering mainstream industrial deployment.

items.title: Flexiv launches new ‘adaptive robots’ for industrial automation
items.summary: Flexiv introduced two novel adaptive robots to advance industrial automation capabilities. These new machines feature enhanced tactile sensing and advanced physical AI functionalities. The company unveiled the Enlight robotic arm and the Mico dual-arm robotic platform. Both units represent a notable advancement in force-controlled robotics and embodied intelligence. The flagship model, the Enlight, is described as a seven-axis adaptive robot.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Flexiv launches new 'adaptive robots' for industrial automation." The report details Flexiv's introduction of two novel adaptive robotic systems designed to significantly enhance current capabilities within the field of industrial automation. These advanced machines incorporate both enhanced tactile sensing and complex physical artificial intelligence functionalities. The company unveiled two specific models: the Enlight robotic arm and the Mico dual-arm robotic platform, representing advancements in force-controlled robotics and embodied intelligence. The flagship unit mentioned is the Enlight, which functions as a seven-axis adaptive robot system. This development signals a measurable progression toward more nuanced and versatile physical interactions within automated industrial settings.

items.title: Limitless Labs raises $20 million to expand AI platform for CNC programming and precision manufacturing
items.summary: Limitless Labs secured $20 million in a Series A funding round. This capital infusion is earmarked for expanding the company's physical AI platform. The company specializes in developing AI software tailored for CNC programming and precision manufacturing processes. Dell Technologies Capital and Square Peg co-led this successful fundraising round. Further investment participation came from Grove Ventures and Meron Capital, among others.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Limitless Labs raises $20 million to expand AI platform for CNC programming and precision manufacturing." This report details the successful completion of a significant funding round for the artificial intelligence firm. The company announced securing twenty million dollars in a Series A financing round. The capital is designated for augmenting the development of its physical AI platform, which specializes in creating tailored artificial intelligence software for complex CNC programming and advanced precision manufacturing workflows. Dell Technologies Capital and Square Peg acted as co-leads on this fundraising effort, with supplementary investment coming from Grove Ventures and Meron Capital among other participants. This substantial influx of capital signals continued sector confidence in specialized industrial automation platforms utilizing generative AI technologies.

items.title: People want robots in warehouses and factories, not hospitals or schools, Hexagon study finds
items.summary: Hexagon's global Robot Generation study reveals public acceptance of robots in workplaces, with both adults and children showing openness to their use. Adults generally expressed a preference for robots performing physical or repetitive tasks. However, the report indicates that people are setting specific boundaries regarding appropriate robot deployment locations. Notably, respondents showed less enthusiasm or cited reservations about using robots in sensitive environments like hospitals or schools. Overall, public desire favors robots augmenting industrial and factory settings rather than care or educational ones.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "People want robots in warehouses and factories, not hospitals or schools, Hexagon study finds." This report details public attitudes regarding the integration of robotics into various sectors of daily life, revealing a strong preference for industrial applications over care or educational settings. The global Robot Generation study assessed openness to robot use among adults and children alike. While respondents indicated general acceptance of robots augmenting physical or repetitive tasks in workplaces, reservations were noted specifically concerning sensitive environments such as hospitals and schools. This indicates public desire leans toward utilizing robotic technology primarily within industrial and factory automation contexts. Industry analysis suggests a continued focus on developing robust solutions for manufacturing augmentation going forward.

items.title: Teradar enters paid evaluation program with German automaker for terahertz vehicle sensing technology
items.summary: Teradar is participating in a paid technical evaluation program with a German automaker. This assessment focuses on validating Teradar's terahertz (THz) vision technology. The goal is to integrate the THz sensing for advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). The technology aims to improve upon limitations found in current sensors like cameras, radar, and lidar. Successful testing will advance autonomous driving platforms utilizing THz capabilities.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Teradar enters paid evaluation program with German automaker for terahertz vehicle sensing technology." The report details a paid technical assessment underway between Teradar and a major German automotive manufacturer concerning the company's terahertz vision technology. This collaboration is specifically focused on validating the integration of THz sensing capabilities into advanced driver assistance systems or ADAS platforms. The initiative aims to address known limitations present in existing sensor modalities, such as standard cameras, radar, and lidar units. Successful completion of these rigorous tests will serve to advance the development trajectories for autonomous driving platforms by incorporating terahertz-based data streams. Industry observers anticipate that positive results from this evaluation could accelerate the commercial viability of next-generation vehicular sensing hardware.

items.title: Orbbec shows AI-powered vision systems at Automate 2026
items.summary: Orbbec showcased industrial-grade 3D cameras at Automate 2026. Their presentation focused on applications for complex automation scenarios. A key feature highlighted was the integration of Artificial Intelligence into their systems. This AI enhances robotic perception capabilities significantly. Information regarding this showcase was initially published by The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Orbbec shows AI-powered vision systems at Automate 2026." This coverage details Orbbec’s presentation of advanced industrial-grade three-dimensional cameras demonstrating significant advancements in automation capabilities through artificial intelligence integration. At the event, Orbbec showcased their hardware focusing on complex automation scenarios that require high levels of environmental understanding. The core presentation emphasized how integrated Artificial Intelligence modules enhance the inherent perception abilities of robotic systems, allowing for more nuanced object recognition and operational adaptability within industrial settings. This market focus signals a continued trend toward embedding sophisticated artificial intelligence directly into physical machine vision infrastructure across various manufacturing sectors.

items.title: Australian rescue team uses AI-powered drone to find lost hikers – video
items.summary: Two men in their 20s were located using an artificial intelligence-powered drone that employed thermal imaging. The hikers had deviated from the established walking track in Kosciuszko national park, New South Wales. They were found approximately half a kilometre off the main trail on Tuesday. This incident marked the first deployment of the FRNSW drone's AI detection system for missing person rescues. Authorities successfully located the individuals within five hours of them going missing.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Australian rescue team uses AI-powered drone to find lost hikers – video," a rescue operation in New South Wales utilized artificial intelligence technology to locate missing individuals in a national park setting. Two men in their twenties were found on Tuesday after they wandered off the designated walking track within Kosciuszko national park. The advanced drone employed thermal imaging capabilities, allowing authorities to pinpoint the hikers' location approximately half a kilometre from the main trail. This deployment marked the initial use of the Fire and Rescue NSW drone’s specialized AI detection system for missing person recovery efforts. Experts suggest this successful operation signals a growing integration of sophisticated autonomous technology into emergency response protocols across various sectors.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: 🍺 ros2-jazzy is now available on Homebrew!
items.summary: All three ROS 2 distributions, including the new Jazzy release, are now natively available on Homebrew-macOS via a specific tap. Installing with `brew install ros2-jazzy` grants immediate access to key components like Gazebo Harmonic (gz-sim8), ros2_control, MoveIt 2, and Nav2. This setup runs directly on macOS without requiring Docker or Virtual Machines. These installations are not static snapshots, ensuring they remain synchronized with upstream developments. Furthermore, patched tutorials for MoveIt 2 and TurtleBot4 are available specifically for the Jazzy distribution on macOS users.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: 🍺 ros2-jazzy is now available on Homebrew!". The announcement details the native availability of all three ROS 2 distributions, including the recent Jazzy release, through a dedicated Homebrew tap for macOS users. Installation is accomplished via the command brew install ros2-jazzy, which grants direct access to crucial packages such as Gazebo Harmonic, ros2_control, MoveIt 2, and Nav2 without reliance on Docker or virtual machines. A key finding is that these installations maintain synchronization with upstream developments rather than remaining static snapshots. Additionally, specific patched tutorials for MoveIt 2 and the TurtleBot4 have been published for Jazzy users operating on macOS environments. This development streamlines complex robotic simulation setup across desktop operating systems, accelerating accessibility for robotics software deployment.

items.title: Video Friday: Give Robots a Hand
items.summary: Video Friday features robotics videos demonstrating various robot capabilities, such as advanced hand mechanisms, safe human interaction approaches ("SoftMimic"), and dynamic obstacle navigation by robots like Digit. Highlighted topics include the DARPA Lift Challenge and a look at fire-fighting robotics. Furthermore, content explores future potential, including using AI to understand animal language from sources like whale behavior research. The weekly selection also provides upcoming event calendars for major robotics conferences.
items.audio_script: From IEEE Spectrum - Robotics, "Video Friday: Give Robots a Hand." This report compiles various demonstrations showcasing current and emerging capabilities across multiple fields of robotics technology. The feature presented numerous examples, including advanced robotic hand mechanisms designed for complex manipulation, and systems employing approaches like "SoftMimic" to ensure safe physical interaction with human subjects. Content also covered autonomous navigation feats, such as those demonstrated by robots tackling dynamic obstacles, alongside specialized applications such as fire-fighting robot deployment and participation in challenges like the DARPA Lift Challenge. Further explorations included research into using artificial intelligence to decipher complex patterns in animal behavior, referencing sources including whale vocalization analysis. The curated material concluded with listings for forthcoming major robotics conferences, signaling continued industry development in these areas.
